description: Class 6 Science interactive (NCERT Curiosity) demonstrating threshing by hand — a farmer beats bundled crop against a hard surface to separate grain from stalks. Locked to the By Hand method with a 4-step animated demo and drag-to-sort Compare board.
subject: Science
topic: Threshing — separating grain from stalks by hand
concepts: Separation of grain from harvested crop
Choosing a method by scale, cost, and effort

1.0.0: Locked to byHand method — no method toggle, no section nav.
Removed intro paragraph, Explore tagline, and method switcher.
Removed initialMethod and showSectionNav props (not applicable).

width.description: Maximum width in pixels. The tool is responsive and self-sizes up to this width.

height.description: Optional minimum height in pixels. 0 = let content determine height (recommended).

initialSection.description: The tool only has the Tools section.

showPlayPause.description: Show/hide the play/pause control on the Tools-section demos.

additionalProps.description: Agent config may be passed nested here (same pattern as the other tools). Values here override the top-level parameters of the same name. Step copy and Compare-board cards are defined internally and are not agent-configurable.

tools.title: Tools — Explore
tools.intro: Explore — see threshing in action before we name the parts.
tools.description: An animated 4-step demo of the By Hand method, followed by a side-by-side CompareView and a drag-to-sort Compare board. The student drags four statement cards onto the By Hand or By Machine side; correct drops lock with a reason, wrong drops bounce back with a hint, and completing it triggers confetti and a summary.

compareBoard.description: Drag each card to the side it best fits. Each card is correct for exactly one side.

cards.text: Threshes a whole field in a day

components: Pill UIButton kit (contained / highlight / outlined / text)
Inline stroke icons (no external icon library)
designSystem.notes: Imports only react — no external dependencies, no Tailwind CDN.

defaultUsage.description: Show the animated By Hand demo with play/pause.

noControls.description: Show the By Hand demo without play/pause (auto-plays through steps).

ncertAlignment.keyConceptTaught: Threshing separates grain from the cut, dried stalks of a harvested crop. It can be done by hand (beating bundles against a hard surface) or by machine (a powered drum). Both do the same job; the better choice depends on the amount, cost, and effort.
learningObjectives: Define threshing as separating grain from harvested stalks
Describe the by-hand method and the by-machine method
Compare the two methods by amount, cost, fuel, and effort
Justify which method suits a given situation (small amount vs whole field)
Connect threshing to other separation methods in the chapter
